subject: History
chapter: The Making of a Global World

activities_projects.title: Global Food Origins Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify the original homes of common foods
materials: Pictures of potato, maize, tomato, chilies
steps: Collect or draw pictures of common foods like potatoes and chilies
Identify that these were 'unknown' to our ancestors until the Americas were discovered
Paste them on a map showing their journey from the Americas to Asia and Europe
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that global trade changed what we eat
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of food origins (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ancient Silk Route Drawing
steps: Draw a desert or sea scene with traders carrying silk, pottery, and spices
Label the regions being connected: China, India, and Rome (Europe)
Color the route to show land and sea paths
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the Silk Route as an early global link
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and correct labeling of goods (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'El Dorado' Legend Poster
steps: Research the fabled city of gold 'El Dorado'
Make a poster showing why Europeans were desperate to find gold in South America
Write a slogan that an explorer might use to recruit sailors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the economic drive behind discovery
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance to the historical theme (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Traveler's Diary Entry
activities_projects.objective: Imagine life on a historical trade ship
steps: Imagine you are a sailor in the 16th century
Write a 5-line diary entry about seeing a new land or trading spices for the first time
Describe one item you brought back with you
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Empathizes with the experiences of early global travelers

activities_projects.title: World Map: The Great Exchange
activities_projects.objective: Map the movement of animals and plants
steps: Draw arrows to show horses and cattle moving from Europe to America
Draw arrows to show potatoes and corn moving from America to Europe
Circle the Atlantic Ocean as the main bridge of this exchange
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the biological exchange between continents
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the exchange directions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Indentured Labour 'Ad' Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the migration of Indian workers
steps: Draw a poster showing recruiters looking for workers for tea or sugar plantations
Mention the names of two islands where they went (e.g., Mauritius, Fiji)
Include the word 'Contract' or 'Girmit' on the poster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the forced movement of labor in the 19th century

activities_projects.title: Industrial Revolution Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Sequence the rise of mass production
steps: Draw a simple timeline of three events: 1. Rise of factories, 2. Expansion of railways, 3. The first Assembly Line (Henry Ford)
Add a small drawing of a steam engine or a car next to the dates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Builds basic chronological awareness of industrialization
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct order of historical periods (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mass Production Observation
steps: Watch a short clip or look at a picture of a car factory
Write down three benefits of making things in a fast-moving line
Draw a simple diagram of a conveyor belt
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the basic idea of the Fordist production model

activities_projects.title: The Global Word Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms from the chapter
steps: Find 8 words: SILK, SPICE, TRADE, GOLD, POTATO, FACTORY, CANAL, GLOBAL
Define any two words found in your own sentences

activities_projects.title: Currency Identification Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of the Dollar and Gold
steps: Draw a symbol of a Gold bar and a Dollar sign ($)
Write one sentence explaining that after World War II, the Dollar became the most important currency
Write the name of the 'World Bank' next to the symbols
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Basic introduction to post-war global finance
